title: Clean Energy Financing Access Gaps In Low-Income Countries
summary: Build a verified workflow for mapping clean energy financing gaps at sub-national scale in low-income countries, with explicit investment-flow data limitations, financing-structure barrier documentation, and the distinction between financing volume and financing access.
why_it_matters: IRENA estimates clean energy investment in low-income countries needs to increase 7-fold to meet SDG7 and climate goals. Distributed renewables face the largest gaps — enterprises serving rural populations encounter a 'missing middle' too large for microfinance, too small for commercial project finance, and too risky for most private investors. Concessional finance flows disproportionately to utility-scale projects in middle-income countries. Without verified sub-national financing-gap analysis, concessional-finance allocation cannot be targeted where it would most close energy-access gaps.

success_metrics.target: Every financing-gap claim documents whether data is national aggregate or sub-national, and explicitly states where district-level financing data is absent.
success_metrics.validation_method: Schema validation and reviewer spot-checks against investment-flow data documentation.

success_metrics.target: No financing-gap analysis is merged without documenting which structural barriers (missing middle, currency risk, transaction costs, perceived risk) limit financing access.
success_metrics.validation_method: Reviewer confirms financing-structure barrier documentation for each analyzed context.

success_metrics.target: Every financing claim documents that increasing finance volume alone does not close access gaps — structure, instruments, and intermediation all matter.
success_metrics.validation_method: Red-team reviewer confirms volume-vs-access distinction in every financing-gap claim.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, financing-structure safety review, and replication for quantitative outputs.
